about.description: A native music streaming client for Linux, designed for audiophiles who need bit-perfect Hi-Fi playback without browser sample rate limitations.
about.author: Author
about.signatureDetail: Hatred towards all those companies that discriminate against the Linux community and don't provide us with a decent client for their product.

tooltip.title: The Hi-Fi Wizard
tooltip.desc: If you haven't already, he can help you set up bit-perfect on your DAC

welcome.title: Guided DAC Setup
welcome.subtitle: This wizard helps you configure your DAC for bit-perfect playback on Linux.
welcome.intro:
QBZ will not modify your system automatically.
Each step shows the exact commands to run, explains what they do, and lets you decide when to proceed.
welcome.bulletResponsibility: You are responsible for creating, keeping, and restoring your own backups
welcome.bulletNoGuarantee: QBZ follows official documentation and recommended practices, but cannot guarantee results on systems with:
welcome.bulletHelper: This guide is provided as a helper, not a system recovery tool
welcome.systemIssue2: Manually modified PipeWire, Pulse, or WirePlumber setups
welcome.systemIssue3: Commands skipped or executed out of order
welcome.recovery: If anything goes wrong, you can restore your previous configuration using the backup created during this process.
welcome.readyText:
When you're ready, continue step by step.
Nothing will run unless you run it.
welcome.checkbox: I understand and accept these terms

precheck.title: Check Audio Stack
precheck.subtitle: Confirm PipeWire and WirePlumber are running.
precheck.inactiveWarning: If any service is inactive, stop here and fix it before continuing.
precheck.checkbox: I ran this command and services are active
precheck.installSubtitle: Select your distribution to see the install command.
precheck.otherHint: Ensure these packages (or equivalents) are installed: pipewire, pipewire-pulse, wireplumber, alsa-utils

detectDac.title: Identify Default DAC
detectDac.subtitle: We need the exact PipeWire node name.
detectDac.step1: Find your DAC under 'Audio > Sinks'. Note the number (e.g., 46).
detectDac.inputWarning: This must match exactly. One character off will break the rule.

validation.invalidFormat: Invalid format - should start with alsa_output. or alsa_input.
validation.pciDac: Internal audio - bit-perfect depends on hardware

query.disclaimer: If these values don't match your DAC specs, check the manufacturer documentation.

backup.title: Create Backup
backup.subtitle: Required before any changes.
backup.hint: You are responsible for keeping this backup until you verify everything works.

pipewireConfig.title: Allow Multiple Sample Rates
pipewireConfig.subtitle: Enables automatic switching without forcing 48kHz.
pipewireConfig.explanation: By default, PipeWire locks to 48kHz. This config allows your DAC to switch to the track's native sample rate.
pipewireConfig.selectRates: Select sample rates your DAC supports:
pipewireConfig.noRatesHint: No rates selected. Default values will be used.

pulseConfig.title: Choose Apps for Bit-Perfect
pulseConfig.subtitle: Add QBZ and any players you want without resampling. Uses PipeWire stream rules (applies to all audio APIs).
pulseConfig.warning: Best results: use app-scoped bit-perfect only. Global Pulse bit-perfect can break browsers and apps that output fixed 48kHz.

wireplumberConfig.title: Target Your DAC Only
wireplumberConfig.subtitle: Apply rules only to the detected sink.
wireplumberConfig.rulesNote: These rules apply only to the detected DAC.

restart.title: Restart Audio Services
restart.subtitle: Required to apply changes.
restart.hint: Audio may briefly stop. This is normal.

verify.title: Verify Sample-Rate Switching
verify.subtitle: Play different sample rates and confirm RATE changes.
verify.instructions:
1. Open a terminal and run: pw-top
2. In QBZ, play a 44.1kHz track
3. Check that RATE shows 44100
4. Play a 96kHz track
5. Check that RATE shows 96000
verify.success: RATE should change to match each track's sample rate.
verify.rollbackHint: Run these commands to restore your previous configuration:
verify.postCloseHint: Close this wizard first, then verify in a terminal. These instructions are for reference — you can always re-open the wizard from Settings > Audio.

done.title: Setup Complete
done.subtitle: Your DAC is configured for bit-perfect playback.

error.title: Need Help?
error.body: If something went wrong, check these resources:

degraded.title: Service degraded
degraded.description: Qobuz servers are experiencing issues. Playback may require more retries or lower quality.

library.playlistOfflineMode: Playlist management is disabled in offline mode. You can still play your local tracks.

welcome.title: Remote Control Setup
welcome.subtitle: Control QBZ from another device
welcome.intro: This guide will help you set up remote control so you can control QBZ from your phone, tablet, or another device on the same network.
welcome.feature4: Install as PWA on your device

enable.title: Enable Remote Control
enable.subtitle: Turn on the local API server
enable.portNote: The default port is 8182. You can change it if needed, but you'll need to update it during pairing.

https.title: Enable Secure Connection
https.subtitle: Required for PWA installation
https.intro: HTTPS is required to install the remote control as a Progressive Web App (PWA) on your phone or tablet.
https.step2: QBZ will generate a self-signed certificate
https.whyReason1: Modern browsers require HTTPS for PWA installation
https.whyReason2: Secure connection protects your pairing token
https.whyReason3: Required for service workers and offline features

certificate.title: Install Certificate
certificate.subtitle: Trust the QBZ certificate on your device
certificate.intro: Since QBZ uses a self-signed certificate, you need to install and trust it on your controlling device.
certificate.step1: Copy the certificate URL from QBZ settings
certificate.step2: Open the URL on your controlling device's browser
certificate.step4: Trust the certificate for web browsing
certificate.iosStep1: Open the certificate URL in Safari
certificate.iosStep3: Go to Settings → Profile Downloaded → Install
certificate.iosStep4: Go to Settings → General → About → Certificate Trust Settings
certificate.iosStep5: Enable full trust for the QBZ certificate
certificate.macosStep1: Open the certificate URL in Safari
certificate.macosStep3: Keychain Access will open - add to login keychain
certificate.macosStep5: Set "When using this certificate" to "Always Trust"
certificate.windowsStep1: Open the certificate URL in any browser
certificate.windowsStep5: Select "Place all certificates in: Trusted Root Certification Authorities"
certificate.androidStep1: Open the certificate URL in Chrome
certificate.androidStep4: Select "VPN and apps" or "Wi-Fi" as usage type

pair.title: Pair Your Device
pair.subtitle: Connect to QBZ
pair.intro: Now connect your controlling device to QBZ using the QR code or manual entry.
pair.qrStep2: Open the QBZ Control URL on your device
pair.qrStep3: Scan the QR code to auto-fill connection details
pair.manualStep1: Note the Local URL shown in QBZ
pair.manualStep2: Open this URL on your controlling device
pair.manualStep3: Enter the URL and token manually
pair.trouble1: Make sure both devices are on the same network
pair.trouble2: Check that the firewall allows port 8182
pair.trouble3: Verify HTTPS is enabled and certificate is trusted

done.title: Setup Complete!
done.subtitle: You can now control QBZ remotely
done.pwaHint: For the best experience, install QBZ Control as a PWA:
done.pwaStep1: Open the control URL on your device
done.pwaStep2: Use "Add to Home Screen" or "Install App"
done.pwaStep3: Launch from your home screen for fullscreen experience

reportIssue.title: Report an issue
reportIssue.intro: To make bug and issue resolution easier, it's recommended to share the application logs. We have been careful to mask the logs we send so no sensitive data is included. The process is manual, never automatic, so you can verify what is being shared.
reportIssue.hideNextTime: Do not show this again, you can always access the log screen on Settings > Developer Mode > View logs

settings.title: Settings

audio.title: Audio
audio.streamingQualityDesc: Maximum quality for streaming and playback cache
audio.outputDeviceDesc: Select your preferred audio output device. Devices shown are from the selected backend.
audio.audioDeviceDesc: Select your preferred audio output device. Devices shown are from the selected backend.
audio.exclusiveModeDesc: Lock audio device for exclusive use
audio.exclusiveModeNotAvailable: Exclusive mode is only available with ALSA Direct backend. PipeWire and PulseAudio are multiplexed audio servers and cannot provide true exclusive access.
audio.exclusiveModeHelp: ALSA Direct only. Locks the audio device so no other application can use it. Prevents system sounds from interfering with playback. Not available with PipeWire/PulseAudio since they are multiplexed audio servers.
audio.exclusiveModeMacosDesc: Lock audio device for exclusive use and automatically adjust sample rate for bit-perfect playback
audio.exclusiveModeMacosHelp: Prevents system sounds/other apps from interfering with playback and switches the sample rate of the output device to match playback using CoreAudio.
audio.exclusiveModeMacosAvailable: Exclusive mode is available with System Audio on macOS.
audio.exclusiveModeUnavailableMultiplexed: Exclusive mode is only available with ALSA Direct or macOS System Audio. PipeWire and PulseAudio are multiplexed audio servers and cannot provide true exclusive access.
audio.exclusiveModeUnavailableGeneric: Exclusive mode is only available with ALSA Direct or macOS System Audio.
audio.dacPassthroughDesc: Bypass system mixer for bit-perfect output
audio.dacPassthroughNote: Refers to decoded audio content. DAC displays may show transport format, not content resolution.
audio.dacPassthroughDisabledDesc: DAC Passthrough uses pw-metadata to force PipeWire sample rates. It is only compatible with the PipeWire backend.
audio.dacPassthroughExtendedDesc: When enabled, overrides the quality setting above if your audio device doesn't support the selected sample rate. Prevents resampling to guarantee bit-perfect playback.
audio.dacPassthroughHelp: PipeWire only. Uses pw-metadata to set clock.force-rate, forcing PipeWire to switch its graph sample rate to match each track. Without this, PipeWire resamples everything to its default rate (usually 48kHz). When enabled, a 96kHz track plays at native 96kHz. Also overrides the quality setting if your DAC doesn't support the track's rate.
audio.pwForceBitperfectHelp: Requires DAC Passthrough. In addition to forcing the sample rate, this also forces PipeWire's quantum (buffer size) to match the track. This provides the most bit-perfect playback possible through PipeWire, but may cause audio glitches on some systems. The rate and quantum are reset when playback stops so other apps aren't affected.
audio.pwForceBitperfectDesc: Forces PipeWire quantum and sample rate to match the track, resetting on stop. Requires DAC Passthrough.
audio.pwForceBitperfectNote: Experimental. May cause audio glitches on some systems. Only affects PipeWire backend.
audio.allowQualityFallbackDesc: When enabled, automatically tries a lower quality tier if the requested one is unavailable. When disabled, playback or downloads fail instead of silently downgrading.
audio.allowQualityFallbackHelp: Off by default. If Qobuz can't serve Hi-Res for a track, QBZ will report an error instead of quietly falling back to CD quality. Enable this if you prefer uninterrupted playback over strict quality guarantees.
audio.skipSinkSwitchDesc: Prevents QBZ from changing the system default sink between tracks. Use this if you route audio through JACK, qjackctl, or a DAW.
audio.skipSinkSwitchHelp: When enabled, QBZ will not call pactl set-default-sink on stream creation. This preserves any external audio routing you have configured (e.g., JACK, qjackctl, Reaper). Incompatible with DAC Passthrough.
audio.skipSinkSwitchNote: External routing mode. QBZ will not change the system audio output device. Make sure your routing is configured before playback.
audio.syncAudioOnStartupDesc: Reload audio configuration from database when the app starts. Enable if your audio device or settings appear incorrect after launching.
audio.audioBackendDesc: Choose audio system: Auto (recommended), PipeWire (modern), ALSA Direct (bit-perfect, exclusive), or PulseAudio (legacy).
audio.limitQualityToDeviceDesc: When enabled, limits streaming quality to your DAC's maximum sample rate. Prevents requesting formats your device can't play natively.
audio.maxSampleRateDesc: Select your DAC's maximum supported sample rate. Tracks above this rate will be requested at a lower quality from Qobuz.
audio.alsaPluginDesc: hw: Bit-perfect, exclusive. plughw: Auto-convert. pcm: Most compatible.
audio.hardwareVolumeDesc: Experimental: Controls DAC volume via ALSA mixer. Some DACs don't support this - disable for maximum compatibility. If it fails, playback continues normally.
audio.flatpakWarningDesc: PipeWire cannot guarantee bit-perfect playback in sandboxed environments due to daemon access restrictions.
audio.flatpakRecommendedDesc: Switch to ALSA Direct backend for true bit-perfect audio.
audio.alsaUtilsDesc: The alsa-utils package provides hardware enumeration tools that QBZ uses to detect bit-perfect capable devices (hw:X,Y).
audio.alsaUtilsNotInstalled: Without it, only generic ALSA devices are shown, and you won't see the BP badge for true bit-perfect outputs.
audio.systemConfigured: Your system is properly configured for bit-perfect device detection.
audio.deviceNotListed: If you don't see your device listed as bit-perfect, ensure it's connected and recognized by your system.
audio.resetDesc: Restores all audio and playback settings to their factory defaults. Useful if you experience audio issues.
audio.resetConfirmDesc: This will reset all audio output, backend, and playback settings to defaults. Playback will be stopped.
audio.resetSuccess: Audio and playback settings have been reset to defaults.

reserveDac.label: Reserve DAC for QBZ while running
reserveDac.description: Tells the system audio service to leave this DAC alone while QBZ is open. Recommended if other apps occasionally interrupt your listening with notification sounds. Off by default.
reserveDac.statusActive: Active — system audio is staying clear.
reserveDac.statusContested: Another app currently holds this DAC at higher priority. Will retry when it releases.
reserveDac.statusUnavailable: D-Bus unavailable in this environment. Reservation cannot be applied.
reserveDac.wirePlumberNote: If your system has a custom WirePlumber configuration that overrides device reservation, this setting may have no effect.

playback.title: Playback
playback.gaplessDesc: Seamless transitions between cached tracks with matching format
playback.autoplayBehaviorDesc: When enabled, plays next track in the source. When disabled, stops after each track
playback.showContextIcon: Show track playing context in player
playback.showContextIconTooltip: Shows the context from which the current track is being played (album, artist, playlist, etc.)
playback.persistSessionDesc: Remember your queue and current view when restarting (track shown paused at 0:00)
playback.resumePlaybackPositionDesc: When restoring a session, seek to where you left off instead of starting the track from the beginning
playback.streamUncachedDesc: Start playback faster when track is not in cache. Seeking may be limited during initial buffering.
playback.initialBufferDesc: Seconds to buffer before starting playback ({seconds}s)
playback.streamingOnlyDesc: Disables temporary cache. Not recommended without a fast connection. Offline library tracks always play first.
playback.streamBufferDesc: Start playback faster when track is not in cache. Seeking may be limited during initial buffering.
playback.streamBufferSeconds: Seconds to buffer before starting playback ({seconds}s)
playback.disableCacheDesc: Disables temporary cache. Not recommended without a fast connection. Offline library tracks always play first.
playback.qualityFallbackDesc: What to do when Qobuz servers can't serve the track at any quality

appearance.title: Appearance
appearance.albumHeaderGradientDesc: Use the album cover's dominant color as a backdrop in album detail views.
appearance.autoThemeDesc: Generate a color theme from your system wallpaper or a custom image
appearance.autoThemeExperimental: Experimental: theme may not match your system exactly.
appearance.autoThemeFailedHint: We recommend choosing a different theme or generating one from a custom image.
appearance.sidebarPlaylistCollage: Show playlist cover collages in sidebar
appearance.sidebarPlaylistCollageDesc: Render a 2×2 thumbnail of track covers next to each playlist. Disable on low-end machines to skip the extra images.
appearance.useSystemTitleBarDesc: Replace QBZ custom title bar with your system's native window decorations. Changing this will restart the app.
appearance.hideTitleBarDesc: Hide the title bar completely (for tiling window manager users)
appearance.searchInTitleBarDesc: Show the search field in the title bar instead of the sidebar
appearance.navInTitleBarDesc: Show Discover, Library, and Local Library buttons in the title bar
appearance.navInTitleBarPositionDesc: Where to place the navigation buttons in the title bar
appearance.windowControlsPositionDesc: Place the window control buttons on the left or right side of the title bar
appearance.windowControlsStyleDesc: Choose the shape of the window control buttons
appearance.windowControlsSizeDesc: Adjust the size of the window control buttons
appearance.windowControlsColorPresetDesc: Choose a color scheme for the window control buttons
appearance.matchSystemChromeDesc: Round the window corners and add a subtle edge so QBZ blends with your desktop theme. Takes effect after restarting the app (the window is rebuilt transparent so the corners don't show white).
appearance.matchSystemChromeRestart: Restart QBZ to apply the window chrome change.
appearance.showWindowControlsDesc: Show minimize, maximize, and close buttons in the title bar. Disable if your window manager handles these.
appearance.showPurchasesDesc: Show the Purchases section in the sidebar for browsing and downloading your purchased music

immersive.title: Immersive
immersive.backgroundModeDesc: Controls how the blurred artwork background is rendered in immersive mode.

backgroundModes.fullDesc: GPU-accelerated shader with animated blur, color shifts, and breathing effects.
backgroundModes.liteDesc: Pre-blurred image with smooth CSS animation. Best for multi-monitor setups.
backgroundModes.offDesc: Solid dark color. Minimal GPU usage.

immersive.perfDegraded: QBZ detected performance issues with the immersive view. Some effects have been temporarily disabled. You can re-enable them from Settings > Appearance.

immersiveFps.title: Animation Frame Rate
immersiveFps.summary: Per-animation FPS control for immersive mode
immersiveFps.desc: Configure frame rate per animation. Lower values save CPU. Requires re-entering immersive mode to apply.

appearance.startupPageDesc: Choose which page to show when the app starts
appearance.hardwareAccelerationDesc: Enable GPU-accelerated rendering. Disabled by default for maximum compatibility. If QBZ crashes on startup after enabling, run: QBZ_HARDWARE_ACCEL=0 qbz
appearance.forceX11Desc: Use X11 (XWayland) instead of native Wayland. Helps with crashes on some NVIDIA/Wayland setups. Requires restart. Override: QBZ_FORCE_X11=0 qbz

tray.title: System Tray
tray.enableTrayDesc: Show icon in system tray (requires restart)
tray.enableTrayDescMacos: Show icon in menu bar (requires restart)
tray.minimizeToTrayDesc: Hide window to tray when minimizing
tray.closeToTrayDesc: Hide window to tray instead of quitting

iconTheme.label: Tray icon variant
iconTheme.desc: Pick a mono glyph to match your panel (Plasma, GNOME's permanently dark top bar) or the full colour vinyl logo

composition.title: Composition
composition.summary: Visual rendering and GPU acceleration settings
composition.helpText: These settings control how QBZ renders the UI. Incorrect settings may cause crashes or poor performance. All options require a restart to take effect.
composition.recoveryNote: If QBZ crashes after changing these settings, launch from terminal with:
composition.fallbackDesc: Using safe defaults. This may cause degraded performance. To reset:
composition.hardwareAccelerationDesc: Enable GPU rendering. Disabling forces software rendering (CPU only). Turn off if you experience crashes or visual artifacts.
composition.forceDmabufDesc: Force DMA-BUF buffer sharing with compositor. May improve performance but can crash on some systems (especially NVIDIA + Wayland).
composition.forceX11Desc: Use XWayland instead of native Wayland. Recommended for NVIDIA GPUs that crash on Wayland.
composition.gdkScaleDesc: GDK_SCALE override for XWayland. Leave empty for auto.
composition.gdkDpiScaleDesc: GDK_DPI_SCALE override for XWayland. Leave empty for auto.
composition.gskRendererDesc: GSK_RENDERER override. Controls how GTK4 renders the UI. Cairo is software-only (safest). GL/NGL use OpenGL. Vulkan uses the Vulkan API.
composition.envVarsDesc: For advanced users. Set these before launching QBZ to override any setting above.
composition.envVarHwAccel1: Force full GPU rendering (bypass all safety)

profiles.title: Rendering profiles
profiles.helpText: Quick presets for mixed-DPI and multi-monitor setups. Apply one and restart QBZ.
profiles.nativeWaylandDesc: Use native Wayland rendering. Best text sharpness, may be heavier on some GPU/compositor combos.
profiles.x11BalancedDesc: Force Xwayland with GDK_SCALE=1 and GDK_DPI_SCALE=1.1 for a balanced size/sharpness tradeoff.
profiles.x11PerformanceDesc: Force Xwayland with neutral scaling (1.0/1.0). Blur background disabled for lower GPU load.
profiles.maxPerformanceDesc: Software rendering (Cairo) with blur disabled. Maximum compatibility, lowest GPU usage. Use if experiencing stutter or crashes.
profiles.applied: Applied profile: {profile}. Restart QBZ to take effect.

titlebar.label: Window titlebar

disabledHint.windowControls: Only applies when QBZ titlebar is active. System and Plasma modes disable this because the OS provides it.
disabledHint.matchSystemChrome: Only applies when QBZ titlebar is active. System and Plasma modes disable this because the OS provides chrome.
disabledHint.searchLocation: Search location does not apply when no titlebar is shown.
disabledHint.titlebarNav: Titlebar navigation does not apply when no titlebar is shown.

titlebar.sandboxCaveat: Plasma mode is unverified in sandboxed builds (Flatpak/Snap). If the system chrome doesn't appear, switch to System mode.

library.title: Library
library.fetchArtistImagesDesc: Show artist photos from Qobuz™ in local library

integrations.title: Integrations

discordRpc.label: Show Discord activity
discordRpc.description: When playing music in QBZ, show the current track in your Discord status. Off by default — toggle on to enable.
discordRpc.overrideHeader: If activity doesn't show in Discord (sandboxed installs)
discordRpc.flatpakNote: Flatpak: run this once, then restart QBZ.
discordRpc.snapNote: Snap: not supported. Install the .deb, .rpm, or .AppImage from GitHub Releases.

integrations.musicbrainzDesc: Enable artist relationships and enhanced metadata from MusicBrainz. QBZ has no telemetry — data is only pulled from MusicBrainz, never sent.
integrations.remoteControlDesc: Control QBZ from another device on the same network.
integrations.remoteControlSecureDesc: Required for PWA installs and HTTPS access. Generates a local certificate.
integrations.remoteControlTokenDesc: Use this token to connect from the web app.
integrations.remoteControlCertDesc: Install and trust this certificate on the controlling device.
integrations.remoteControlRegenerateDesc: This will invalidate the current pairing token. You will need to rescan the QR code.
integrations.remoteControlQrHelp: Scan this QR code with QBZ-Control to pair.
integrations.plexConnectionLore: Access your Plex library directly from QBZ Local Library. This integration supports only local and LAN Plex servers to guarantee direct play.
integrations.plexCollapseHint: This only hides the block. Your settings stay unchanged.
integrations.plexServerUrlHelp: Must be a local or local-LAN URL.
integrations.plexLocalAddressRequired: Use a local or LAN Plex address. Remote Plex endpoints are not supported for this mode.
integrations.plexAuthorizeSectionLore: Authorize QBZ to connect with your Plex server.
integrations.plexAuthorizeHelp: If it does not open automatically, visit https://plex.tv/link.
integrations.plexLinkWithPlexHelp: Take note of this one-time code.
integrations.plexManualTokenToggle: Or enter your Plex X-Token directly
integrations.plexManualTokenHelp: Optional. Not required if the code flow above worked.
integrations.plexGetLibrariesHelp: Re-sync if new libraries were added on your Plex server.
integrations.plexClearCacheRowHelp: Recommended if you notice library inconsistencies.
integrations.plexPocDesc: Experimental local-only integration. Uses direct media parts from Plex without replacing QBZ playback.
integrations.plexLibraryIntegrationNote: Plex music is integrated into Local Library (same queue/player flow, no separate tab).
integrations.plexLocalOnlyDirectPlayNote: Only Plex libraries pointing to local files are supported to guarantee direct play.
integrations.plexMetadataWriteDesc: Disabled by default. Keep off to use local-only metadata edits in QBZ and avoid writing changes back to Plex.
integrations.plexWriteDisabledNotice: Plex metadata write is disabled. Enable it in Settings if you want to edit Plex album metadata.
integrations.plexEasyConnectDesc: Open plex.tv/link in your browser, enter the code shown here, approve QBZ, then return.
integrations.plexStatusAuthPending: Plex sign-in started. Complete authorization with code {code}.
integrations.plexStatusAuthExpired: Plex sign-in code expired. Start the connection again.
integrations.plexStatusDisconnected: Plex connection and cached metadata were cleared.
integrations.plexStatusCacheLoaded: Loaded {count} cached tracks. Refreshing from Plex in background.
integrations.plexStatusPlayingFallback: Playing {title}, but direct play is not confirmed (part: {partKey}).
integrations.plexDisconnectConfirmDesc: This removes the Plex token and clears cached Plex metadata.
integrations.plexClearCacheConfirmDesc: This removes locally cached Plex sections/tracks in QBZ. Your Plex token remains connected.
integrations.qobuzLinkHandlerDesc: Register QBZ as the default app for qobuzapp:// links from your browser.
integrations.qobuzLinkHandlerError: Failed to update link handler registration.
integrations.noProtocolDeviceFoundHint: Make sure devices are on the same network

startupMode.title: Auto-connect Qobuz Connect on startup
startupMode.description: Choose whether Qobuz Connect activates automatically when QBZ launches.
startupMode.localLibraryNote: Remember, Qobuz Connect is not compatible with local library.

storage.title: Storage
storage.sectionSummary: Queue, lyrics, artwork, and metadata caches
storage.queueCacheNote: Queue list storage. Clear if you have playback issues. (This is not the Offline Library cache.)
storage.factoryResetDesc: Permanently deletes all settings, caches, credentials, and user data. You will be logged out and the app will return to its initial state.
storage.factoryResetCheckbox: I understand all data will be permanently deleted and this cannot be undone
storage.factoryResetFinalConfirm: Are you absolutely sure? This will delete all application data including your credentials, settings, caches, and offline library. This action cannot be undone.
storage.imageCacheEnabledDesc: Cache Qobuz album and artist images locally for faster loading
storage.imageCacheClearDesc: Remove all cached Qobuz images from disk

developer.title: Developer Mode
developer.summary: Advanced options for debugging and development
developer.forceDmabufDesc: Override the NVIDIA + Wayland DMA-BUF workaround. Only enable if you know your system doesn't crash. Reset from terminal: qbz --reset-dmabuf
developer.restartRequired: Restart QBZ for this change to take effect
developer.viewLogsDesc: View backend and console logs for debugging
developer.bundleDiagnosticsDesc: Attach a runtime/system snapshot + a 10s cast scan to the terminal paste so one link covers everything.
developer.bugReportHint: Reporting a bug? Upload both tabs and share both URLs — it helps us fix things faster. Thanks!
developer.verboseLogCaptureDesc: Capture all console logs (log/info), not just errors. Uses more memory.
developer.qconnectDevToolsDesc: Show the advanced connection screen button in the player bar

diagnostics.title: System Diagnostics
diagnostics.description: Compare saved settings against runtime state

blacklist.title: Artist Blacklist
blacklist.description: Blacklisted artists are hidden from search results, radio, suggestions, and similar artists.
blacklist.noArtistsHint: To blacklist an artist, go to their page and click the ban icon.
blacklist.disabledWarning: Blacklist is currently disabled. Artists below will appear in results.
blacklist.confirmClearText: This will remove all {count} artists from your blacklist. This action cannot be undone.

offlineLibrary.title: Offline Playback
offlineLibrary.disclaimer: The offline cache is a temporary playback cache for offline listening while you have a valid Qobuz subscription. You do not receive a license to keep or redistribute the content. If your subscription becomes invalid, QBZ will remove all cached content after 30 days.
offlineLibrary.showInLibraryDesc: Display offline cached tracks in the Local Library view
offlineLibrary.repairDesc: Fix offline markers lost during library scans
offlineLibrary.manageCacheDesc: Browse and manage downloaded tracks and albums
offlineLibrary.openFolderDesc: Browse and manage downloaded tracks and albums

updates.title: Updates
updates.checkOnLaunch: Check for new releases on launch
updates.showWhatsNew: Show what's new on new version launch
updates.changelogUnavailable: Could not load changelog for this version

settings.networkCacheWarning: The selected folder is on a network drive.
settings.networkCacheWarningDetail: Cached tracks may not be available when the network is disconnected or in offline mode. Are you sure you want to use this location?

about.title: About
about.checkOnLaunch: Check for new releases on launch
about.showWhatsNew: Show what's new on new version launch

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.musicbrainzDesc: Enable artist relationships and enhanced metadata from MusicBrainz. QBZ has no telemetry — data is only pulled from MusicBrainz, never sent.
metadata.cachesDesc: Queue, lyrics, artwork and metadata caches

toast.failedRemoveOffline: Failed to remove from offline library
toast.noAvailableTracks: No available tracks in queue (offline mode)
toast.failedLookupMusician: Could not look up musician info
toast.refreshingAlbumOffline: Refreshing all tracks from "{album}" for offline
toast.failedLoadAlbumRefresh: Failed to load album for offline refresh
toast.failedPreviousTrack: Failed to go to previous track
toast.failedNextTrack: Failed to go to next track
toast.localTrackSearch: Local track - search for artist in Search
toast.playlistCreatedOffline: Playlist created offline - will sync when back online
toast.enterSearchTerms: Enter album title or artist to search
toast.rateLimited: Rate limited. Please wait a moment and try again.
toast.failedSaveIconVisibility: Failed to save icon visibility preference
toast.awardUnavailable: Award detail not available for this entry.
toast.audioDeviceMissing: Output device '{device}' is no longer available. Using system default.
toast.mixTrimmed: Added {actual} songs to your queue (you asked for {requested}; some merged or capped per album).

topMixes.title: TopQ
topMixes.subtitle: From your top playlists
topMixes.cardDesc: Discover new music from your most-played playlists.
topMixes.algorithmInfo: Powered by the Qobuz suggestion algorithm, seeded from your most-played playlists.
topMixes.emptyPlaylists: No played playlists yet. Play some playlists to build your mix!

updates.runningInsideSandbox: QBZ detected it's running inside a {env} sandbox.

flatpak.intro: There are some considerations to ensure the best experience.

whatYouShouldKnow.heading: What you should know:
whatYouShouldKnow.externalMusicFolderAccess: Access to external music folders (NAS, network mounts) requires manual permission grants
whatYouShouldKnow.audioFeatures: Some audio features may behave differently due to sandbox restrictions
whatYouShouldKnow.settingsPage: The Settings page includes Flatpak-specific commands you can copy and run

flatpak.note: Check the Flatpak Sandbox section in Settings for detailed instructions and copy-paste commands.

snap.intro: Some audio plugs need to be connected manually for the best experience.
snap.note: Run these commands in a terminal, then restart QBZ. This only needs to be done once.

updates.newVersionReleased: A new version of QBZ has been released

updateReminder.doNotNotifyAgain: Do not notify me again about this release
updateReminder.doNotNotifyAboutReleases: Do not notify me about new releases

whatsNew.releaseNotesNotAvailableForVersion: Release notes are not available for this version.

weeklyMixes.title: WeeklyQ
weeklyMixes.subtitle: Based on your weekly listening.
weeklyMixes.cardDesc: Take a weekly journey with a fresh mix every Friday.

mixModal.title: Random queue
mixModal.body: Create a random queue with songs from this collection. How many songs do you want?

queue.tracksRemoved: {count, plural, one {# track removed from queue} other {# tracks removed from queue}}
